item.title: Empathy for money
item.description: Fp: I want to say thank you for everybody who helped or followed my wife and I and our journey from Virginia to minnesota. We are starting to land on our feet and be able to move forward. As a disabled combat veteran, I want to say thank you for everybody who voted for Harris, and everybody who is supporting what this country is actually supposed to be. Trying to move from Tiktok to YouTube. Would appreciate a subscribe. https://youtube.com/@grid.squares?si=cSvuvjp3Hpy1cJ0q
item.dc:creator: JustGridSquares

item.title: What Happened Today #64
item.description: Trump breaks things on purpose — and dares you to stop him. He tanks the markets, targets dissent, opens forests to industry, and turns the federal government into a personal vendetta machine — then smirks from the golf course and tells you to “get rich.” While he defunds disaster prep, mass-fires civil servants, and forces law firms to swear loyalty oaths, Obama and Harris are stepping up. So must we. Please be safe if you're rallying today. Subscribe free at https://govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist LINKS 1. https://apnews.com/article/donald-trump-tariffs-golf-813c7a300021445636f63fddbfd38c83 2. https://www.nbcnews.com/news/world/china-hits-back-trump-34-tariff-us-imports-rcna199665 3. https://www.yahoo.com/news/breaking-doj-seeks-87-month-013635741.html 4. https://www.militarytimes.com/pentagon/2025/04/04/hegseth-to-visit-panama-as-trump-says-us-is-reclaiming-its-canal/ 5. https://www.sfgate.com/national-parks/article/interior-secretary-national-parks-order-20259116.php 6. https://www.mediaite.com/news/trump-administration-mocks-judges-order-to-bring-back-maryland-father-from-salvadoran-prison-even-after-admitting-he-was-deported-in-error/ 7. https://www.cnbc.com/2025/04/04/trumps-extends-tiktok-second-time.html 8. https://www.msn.com/en-ca/entertainment/other/maya-angelou-memoir-holocaust-book-are-among-those-pulled-from-naval-academy-library-in-dei-purge/ar-AA1Ckqc2 9. https://apnews.com/article/irs-layoffs-civil-rights-compliance-trump-musk-d2c5708973cddf91c1a72b392118628c 10. https://www.fox5ny.com/news/columbia-student-records-congress-notice 11. https://abcnews.go.com/US/wireStory/trump-administration-ends-key-grant-program-helps-communities-120506359 12. https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/apr/04/supreme-court-teacher-grants-dei 13. https://thehill.com/policy/energy-environment/5233501-chemical-industry-pollution-exemptions-trump-biden/ 14. https://billingsgazette.com/article_e4f0667f-9c29-5e4d-9092-37de346a5172.html 15. https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2025/04/04/autism-vaccines-nih-research-trump/ 16. https://www.fox5dc.com/news/director-national-museum-african-american-history-culture-leave 17. https://www.wral.com/story/republican-jefferson-griffin-wins-nc-appeals-court-challenge-in-case-contesting-65k-ballots/21944370/ 18. https://thehill.com/regulation/court-battles/5233200-law-firms-judges-trump-executive-order-perkins-coie/ 19. https://www.cnn.com/2025/04/04/politics/obama-harris-rebuke-trump/index.html

item.title: Duval County Jacksonville Florida Showed Up Today to Fight for Our Democracy!
item.description:
Standing side by side with my fellow citizens really did a lot for my mental health today.
We showed up to stand for a country not lost but fighting for it's future against the tyranny of a fascist president and government.
My friends and neighbors are much better than me at making signs. I love my community and neighbors and will fight for equal rights for all and for my friends and neighbors to be able to be their true selves and to feel safe being themselves.
United we can stand up against this tragedy that's happening in our country right now.
Together we can save our children's future.
Get out and be a part of the movement to stop Trump and Musk.
It's an amazing feeling being together. Saying I love you neighbor, I care about your life. Your problems are my problems. I will fight.
Show up America. We can fight this if we stand together.
item.dc:creator: hordesofgourds

item.title: IQ tests are BS
item.description: So here's a little story of my experience with IQ tests. Back in high school the school had to have me take a proctored IQ test due to me having an IEP. They brought in my parents and me to go over the results, because according to the test I was literally 2 points shy of "genius". Thing is. Back then I sucked at math. I cannot stress it enough just how bad I was at math. I had to retake pre-algebra SOOO many times. It wasn't until I was in college and took an algebra course tailored towards those with difficulty doing math that I was finally able to start doing decent at it. Anyways, back in high school people would always ride me about "why aren't you getting this when the test says you're so smart?" They all saw the results of a flawed test of intelligence and expected me to be great at all academic matters.
item.dc:creator: Othagovna

item.title: This beautiful species
item.description: Dik-dik (Madoqua kirkii) "When frightened or disturbed, they make a whistling sound through their nose that sounds like “zik-zik,” and this is probably how they got their name." Source: https://www.awf.org/wildlife-conservation/dik-dik
item.dc:creator: Fulustreka

item.title: The morning after
item.description:
My foster mama cat gave birth last night, and here’s my first look at her babies this morning! They’re all doing perfectly!
Mama is protective but not aggressive at all towards me when I have to handle her babies. This is just best case scenario!!

item.title: Gustave Doré (1832–1883) was a French artist, illustrator, engraver, and sculptor, best known for his dramatic and highly detailed illustrations that brought classic literary works to life.
item.description:
A prodigious talent from a young age, Doré began publishing his work in Paris at just fifteen and quickly rose to prominence for his distinctive style and imaginative vision.
Doré’s career was marked by his collaborations with some of the most influential texts in Western literature.
His illustrations for Dante’s Divine Comedy, Cervantes’ Don Quixote, Milton’s Paradise Lost, Coleridge’s The Rime of the Ancient Mariner, and the Bible are celebrated for their theatrical compositions, intricate line work, and emotional depth.
Through these works, Doré not only captured the essence of the stories but often elevated them, offering new visual dimensions that have influenced artists, filmmakers, and illustrators for generations.
What sets Doré apart is his ability to blend realism with the fantastical.
His art frequently explores the sublime—depicting towering cathedrals, storm-tossed seas, infernal depths, and celestial heights with equal mastery.
He had a remarkable gift for rendering light and shadow, and his engravings often possess a haunting, almost cinematic quality.
Although he worked across multiple disciplines, Doré is best remembered for his wood engravings and steel plate etchings.
His prolific output and signature visual language helped define the aesthetics of 19th-century book illustration, making him one of the most significant illustrators of his time.
Today, Doré’s legacy endures not only in the pages of the books he illustrated but also in the broader canon of visual art.
His influence can be seen in everything from Gothic art and graphic novels to modern fantasy and film.
item.dc:creator: stardustLUST

item.title: Biscuits on biscuits
item.description: My foster mama cat gave birth last night and I just came in to check on her. She is so sweet. Such a good little biscuit-maker! (As a note, I have been inundated with comments and questions and I promise I will get to everyone! May take a couple of days but I am SO appreciative of everyone who has reached out. This has been an amazing experience and I am so happy I got to share it with all of you!!)
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: Trump/Muskrat Protest today Saint Petersburg, Florida .
item.description:
There were hundreds of people on pretty much every street corner of every block, easily thousands of people out protesting. Incredibly proud of my city today.
I love her so much
No Kings

item.title: Memedump + story: An update on the last three insane years.
item.description:
This is the meme that kicked it all off. More memes at the end
A brief history (very brief) of a year's work.
Curve ball half way through.
Now she's over a year old. How time flies.
In the chaos of the last year, people were still reaching out wanting more. So, we just took the dive and did it again. Despite now having a baby to care for and the craziness of the world in general, we took the shot anyway.
GULP
Us busy prepping for the launch (We don't actually have an office - it's just me and Mike at our PCs. But this is what it would look like.)
It launched about a month ago. (Important note: Not my smug baby)
https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/palaeogames/professor-primulas-portfolio-of-palaeontology?ref=9k7w1u
This is a sample of the type of artwork our book has.
Oh, your party only worries about crocs and hags in a swamp environment? Shame.
Here are some example pages from the book.
Chomp chomp.
Our Yi draft design. We're going to adjust it's optional magical rules a little so that it can still poison enemies while being a familiar. Look at him. LOOK AT HIM.
I hope you like the idea of playing as a realistic Neanderthal, as well as dinofolk ofc.
Here's the link if you're interested https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/palaeogames/professor-primulas-portfolio-of-palaeontology?ref=9k7w1u Otherwise, here's some memes to make up for being a shitty ad (sorry).
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sL01P5zsL18&t=1s
Dave Hone at his best (worst).
It's not cannibalism.
Sneaky dnd meme.
If anyone is interested, you can also see our first book here: https://palaeogames.com/ And the Kickstarter here: https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/palaeogames/professor-primulas-portfolio-of-palaeontology?ref=9k7w1u
item.dc:creator: Palaeo
